title: 8 CEBA Settlement and Reconciliation
body_text:
8	CEBA Settlement and Reconciliation
8.1	CEBA Settlement
Financial Institution Settlement
CEBA loan funds are posted in a lump sum to your financial institutionâs Central 1 account by the end of business day on the same day that Central 1 receives the funds from Export Development Canada (EDC).
The lump sum postings can be viewed on the Current Account Services (CAS) Online, identified as âCEBAâ under the Reference ID field. Refer to the Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part for more information.
On the business day after settlement is received in your Central 1 account, the lump sum transaction is posted on the Central 1 Account Activity and Balances report with code FT. See Figure 1. Credit transactions are reported on the AIBC report for BC financial institutions and on the AIOC report for Ontario financial institutions. Any debit transactions are reported with code DM.
Figure 1: Account Activity and Balances Report (AIBC/AIOC)

Customer Settlement
Deposit the funds to your business customersâ accounts after the transactions are reconciled. If there are discrepancies in the funding amounts received from Central 1, contact Client Support Services. See Section
1.2
, Contacts and Websites.
8.2	Reconciling CEBA Transactions
To reconcile CEBA transactions, refer to the funding note on your Credit/Debit Memo Slip (e.g., âFunding Requested on Apr 30, 2020â) for the day you receive the funds from Central 1.
For BC financial institutions, the funding note is on the CHQ4 â Credit/Debit Memo Slip. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: CHQ4 Credit/Debit Memo Slip
For Ontario financial institutions, the funding note is on the ADVC â Credit/Debit Memo Slip. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: ADVC â Credit/Debit Memo Slip
To verify which applications are funded, go to ServiceNow, select
My Companyâs CEBA Application â Submitted to EDC
, and sort your applications by the Funding Request Date to match which applications have been processed (e.g., if the note on your Credit/Debit Memo Slip refers to April 30, 2020, look for all applications in ServiceNow with that Funding Request Date).
8.3	Settlement of Returned EDC Funds
Central 1 will withdraw the funds from your financial institutionâs current account and wire a transfer back to EDC.
For BC financial institutions, the withdrawal is reported on the CHQ4 â Credit/Debit Memo Slip. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: CHQ4 Credit/Debit Memo Slip (Debit Example)
For Ontario financial institutions, the withdrawal is reported on the ADVC â Credit/Debit Memo Slip. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: ADVC â Credit/Debit Memo Slip (Debit Example)
8.4	Settlement of CEBA Loan Repayments
EDC requires that loan repayments are reported to Central 1 and submitted to EDC twice a month. The reporting schedule for loan repayments is shown in the table below.
Customer Makes Payment to Financial Institution
Financial Institution Reports Payments to Central 1
Between the 1st and the 15th of the month
By the end of the 22nd day of that month
Between the 16th and the last day of the month
By the end of the 7th day of the
following
month
Central 1 will debit your financial institutionâs current account for the reported amounts on the 8th and the 23rd day each month and submit the funds to EDC on the same day.
For BC financial institutions, withdrawal of the repayment amount is reported on the CHQ4 â Credit/Debit Memo Slip. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: CHQ4 Credit/Debit Memo Slip (Debit Repayment Example)
For Ontario financial institutions, withdrawal of the repayment amount is reported on the ADVC â Credit/Debit Memo Slip. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: ADVC â Credit/Debit Memo Slip (Debit Repayment Example)
8.5	Settlement of CEBA Loan Administration Fees
EDC distributes CEBA loan administration fees quarterly, based on the outstanding loan amounts. Your financial institution calculates the fees and submits a report to Central 1. See Chapter 5, Reporting CEBA Loan Administration Fees.
EDC sends the total amount of your financial institutionâs reported fees to Central 1 on the 15th day of each quarterly month (March, June, September, and December). Central 1 allocates 87.5% of the reported administration fees to your financial institution and retains the remaining 12.5% as Central 1âs administration fee.
For BC financial institutions, the loan administration fee amount is reported on the CHQ4 â Credit/Debit Memo Slip. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: CHQ4 Credit/Debit Memo Slip (Loan Admin Fee Example)
For Ontario financial institutions, the loan administration fee amount is reported on the ADVC â Credit/Debit Memo Slip. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: ADVC â Credit/Debit Memo Slip (Loan Admin Fee Example)
END
